name: hero-animated-section
role: Motion-led marketing hero. The second of 5 hero variants. Inherits hero-type-only's parallel anatomy (eyebrow + headline + subhead + actions + meta) but adds entrance choreography and optional ambient motion. Five motion modes — word-stagger (headline reveals word-by-word), type-in (character-by-character typewriter), fade-up (block-level entrance translateY), spring-overshoot (initial-overshoot bounce), scroll-parallax (subtle Y-shift on scroll). Three intensities — subtle / default / dramatic. All animations collapse to a single instant fade or no-op under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.

semanticStructure: (optional) <hero-animated-accent> — ambient motion accent (cursor-follow line, drifting mark, etc.)

motionModes.word-stagger: Headline splits into per-word <span data-word> elements. Each word fades + translates-up in sequence with --stagger-step (60ms) delay. Total entrance ~600ms for a 6-word headline. Subhead + CTAs follow with their own stagger after the headline completes.
motionModes.type-in: Headline characters reveal one-at-a-time via animation-delay applied to per-character <span data-char>. Total entrance proportional to character count. Best for short headlines (< 30 chars). Cursor block (--accent ground) flashes at the end then fades.
motionModes.fade-up: Each block (eyebrow, headline, subhead, actions, meta) fades + translates from translateY(12px) to translateY(0) over --duration-entrance-normal. Stagger between blocks via --stagger-step. The default and most universal mode.
motionModes.spring-overshoot: Same block-level entrance as fade-up but with --ease-overshoot bouncing past final position before settling. translateY(20px) → translateY(-4px) → translateY(0). More personality, used in playful aesthetics (arcade, bloom).
motionModes.scroll-parallax: Static entrance + ambient scroll-driven Y-shift. As page scrolls, hero content shifts at a fraction of scroll velocity, giving depth. Requires JS scrollY listener; CSS-only fallback via scroll-driven animations (limited browser support — fallback to no parallax).

intensities.subtle: Reduced animation distance + duration. translateY range 4px → 0. Used in editorial / B2B contexts where motion should be barely noticeable.
intensities.dramatic: translateY range 24px → 0. Duration 720ms. Used in playful / consumer marketing where motion is a feature.

reducedMotionPolicy.rule: WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions). Under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), ALL entrance animations collapse to a single 120ms opacity fade. No translate, no scale, no rotation. Stagger delays collapse to 0.
reducedMotionPolicy.implementation: Top-level @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block resets animation: opacity 120ms ease-out, animation-delay: 0, transform: none. Scroll-parallax mode also disables the scroll listener.
reducedMotionPolicy.ambientAccentMotion: Ambient motion (hero-animated-accent's cursor-follow or drift) also stops entirely under reduced motion — not slowed, stopped.

ariaPatterns.h1Discipline: hero-animated-headline uses <h1>. One h1 per page.
ariaPatterns.ariaLiveOnEntrance: Do NOT use aria-live on the entrance animation. The headline is in the DOM from initial paint; visual entrance is decoration only. AT announces the heading via the page's natural reading order, not via the animation.

name: hero-animated-eyebrow
role: Small uppercase pre-heading with stagger-index='0' (first to enter). Same anatomy as hero-type-only's hero-eyebrow but participates in the parent's entrance choreography via CSS animation-delay derived from data-stagger-index.

meta.staggerCoordination: data-stagger-index='0' makes this the first entrance. Animation-delay = calc(var(--stagger-step, 60ms) * data-stagger-index).
meta.notAHeading:
Same rule as hero-type-only — eyebrow is

, not a heading. The h1 lives on hero-animated-headline.

name: hero-animated-headline
role: The big display headline. h1 by default. Animation behaviour depends on parent's data-motion-mode — word-stagger splits into <span data-word>, type-in splits into <span data-char>, all other modes animate the headline as a single block.

meta.wordSplittingForStagger:
In word-stagger mode, the host wraps each word:

Five tools that compose.

. Each span animates with delay = calc(var(--stagger-step) * (1 + nth-of-type-index)).
meta.charSplittingForTypeIn:
In type-in mode, the host wraps each character:

Five…

. Spaces remain as plain text between groups. Each character animates with delay = calc(50ms * nth-of-type-index).
meta.blockEntranceForOtherModes: fade-up / spring-overshoot / scroll-parallax animate the h1 as a single block. Animation-delay = calc(var(--stagger-step) * 1).
meta.textWrapBalance: text-wrap: balance still applies — but in word-stagger and type-in modes, the wrapping may be affected by per-span layout. Test in dark mode + reduced-motion to ensure no layout shifts.

name: hero-animated-subhead
role: Supporting subtitle with stagger-index='2'. Animates as a single block in all motion modes (not split per-word like the headline). Entrance delayed until after the headline's animation completes (or near-completes — slight overlap is fine).

name: hero-animated-actions
role: CTA cluster with stagger-index='3'. Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system. Entrance is block-level fade-up + translate, NOT per-button stagger (avoids over-busy entrance for users on the page for less than 1s).

name: hero-animated-meta
role: Optional small-print row below the CTAs with stagger-index='4'. Same content options as hero-type-only's hero-meta (disclaimer / social-proof / trust-badges) but with entrance delay matching the choreography.

meta.trustBadgeAltText: Trust-badge images carry alt-text naming the certification. Not aria-hidden — they're meaningful credentials per the hero-type-only contract.

name: hero-animated-accent
role: Ambient motion element — optional decorative animation that runs independently of the entrance choreography. Three registers: cursor-follow (a subtle accent line tracks the mouse cursor at distance), drift (a slowly-drifting accent mark in the hero corner), pulse (a single pulsing accent dot). Always aria-hidden — purely decorative. Disabled entirely under prefers-reduced-motion.

meta.alwaysAriaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true'. Decorative only. The hero's semantic meaning lives in eyebrow + headline + subhead, not in ambient accents.
meta.reducedMotionDisable: Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, the accent stops entirely (display: none or animation-play-state: paused with transform: none). Not slowed — stopped.
meta.cursorFollowImplementation: Requires JS — listens to mousemove on the hero-section and updates --accent-x / --accent-y CSS custom properties. The accent element uses transform: translate(var(--accent-x), var(--accent-y)). Out of scope for v1 CSS-only; host provides the JS.
meta.driftImplementation: Pure CSS animation. Accent slowly orbits or drifts in a Lissajous-like curve over ~30s, infinite. Animation-name follows the choreography choice; default is a 6-keyframe drift.
meta.pulseImplementation: Pure CSS animation. Accent scales 1 → 1.4 → 1 over 2s, infinite. Opacity also breathes 0.6 → 1 → 0.6 for visual softness.
